coredata.dc:title: Duplex metal co-doped carbon quantum dots-based drug delivery system with intelligent adjustable size as adjuvant for synergistic cancer therapy

coredata.dc:description: Carbon quantum dots (CQDs) are extensively applied for bioimaging due to their unique photoluminescence properties, while less attention has been paid to their inherent bio-functions as targeted drug delivery vehicles. To address this limitation, a novel CQDs-based drug delivery system (Arg-Ag@Cu) was constructed using l-arginine as precursor with duplex metal co-doping (Ag&Cu) for further DOX-loading/releasing and synergistic tumor theranostics. Specifically, the functional residues retention ability of CQDs guaranteed the reactive nitrogen species (RNS) replenishment, Ag-doping enhanced the photoluminescence intensity, and Cu2+-functionalizing could act as the anchor for further interparticle self-assembly, resulting in intelligent adjustable CQDs size for increasing the drug-loading capacity. After reaching targeted sites, CQDs could act as NO/ONOO− donors for gas-involving therapy, and Cu2+ could not only generate ROS through chemodynamic therapy (CDT), but also modulate the band structure of as-prepared CQDs for laser-induced photodynamic therapy (PDT) to further replenishing ROS. Simultaneously, with Cu2+ depletion, the structure of Arg-Ag@Cu was disrupted, which guaranteed in situ DOX-releasing. As such, these orchestrated procedures fully exerted the intrinsic bio-functions of CQDs then act as adjuvants in drug delivery systems, thus representing synergistic effects in suppressing tumor progression and migration with minimal side effects, and provides a paradigm of potent CQDs-based nanotheranostics.

ce:caption.ce:simple-para: Preparation and characterization of Arg-Ag CQDs and Arg-Ag@Cu. (A) UV–vis absorption of Arg-Ag CQDs and Arg-Ag@Cu. (B) Fluorescence spectra of Arg-Ag CQDs and (C) Arg-Ag@Cu under different excitation light ranged from 280 to 400 nm. The insert digital images of each sample were taken under white light and 365 nm laser irradiation. (D) The FT-IR spectrum of L-Arg, Arg-Ag CQDs and Arg-Ag@Cu. (E) Typical TEM images of Arg-Ag CQDs (scale bar = 20 nm) and (F) Arg-Ag@Cu (scale bar = 100 nm). The digital images of actual samples and schematic of their structures were inserted, respectively. (G) Element mapping analysis of Arg-Ag@Cu. Red, blue, green, and yellow represent carbon, oxygen, copper and silver element, respectively. And their overlayed picture was also presented (scale bar = 100 nm). (H) Size distribution of Arg-Ag@Cu measured by DLS. (I) Zeta potential analysis of Arg-Ag CQDs and Arg-Ag@Cu and (J) XPS survey of Arg-Ag@Cu. (A colour version of this figure can be viewed online.)

The production and quantification of RNS. (A) Schematic diagram of laser-induced NO production. (B) Using classic Griess Reagent and (C) DAF-FM DA to evaluate the production of NO (scale bar = 100 μm). (D) The quantification of mean fluorescence intensity of DAF-FM DA (NO production) in 4T1 cells after indicated treatment. (E) FCM analysis of the ONOO− generation in 4T1 cells after indicated treatment. (F) The quantitative fluorescence signal intensity of ONOO− detection probe in 4T1 cells. (n = 3, ∗∗p < 0.01, ∗∗∗p < 0.001).

The production of ROS. (A) The schematic diagram of the CDT effect and PDT effect achieved by Arg-Ag@Cu in response to TME. (B) Using dissolved oxygen analyzer detected the extracellular O2 generation. (C) The depletion of DPBF due to 1O2 generation. (D) UV–vis absorption of MB, a ·OH indicator, after incubated with prespecified components. Inset: The digital images of MB degradation after indicated treatment. (E) Fluorescence images of ROS generation in 4T1 cells incubated with Arg-Ag@Cu (scale bar = 25 μm). (F) The quantification of ROS production in 4T1 cells after indicated treatment. (n = 3/group, ∗∗∗p < 0.001, ∗∗∗∗p < 0.0001).

Drug (DOX) loading and MMVs coating to further enhancing tumor-targeted efficiency. (A) Schematic diagram of DOX loading. (B) The UV–vis absorption spectra and (C) PL spectra of DOX, Arg-Ag@Cu and Arg-Ag@Cu-DOX. The inserted photographs were taken under white light or 365 nm laser irradiation, respectively. (D) The specific process of biomimetic construction. TEM image of (E) MMVs (scale bar = 0.2 μm) and (F) MMV-Arg-Ag@Cu (scale bar = 100 nm). (G) The protein profiles of MMVs, MMV-Arg-Ag@Cu, Arg-Ag@Cu and M1 macrophages were determined by SDS-PAGE electrophoresis assay and Coomassie blue staining. (H) Specific membrane proteins (α4 integrins and CD44) in MMVs, MMV-Arg-Ag@Cu and M1 macrophages were determined by western blot assay. The protein signals of β-actin were performed as a control. (I) Size and zeta potential analysis of MMV, Arg-Ag@Cu and MMV-Arg-Ag@Cu. (A colour version of this figure can be viewed online.)

In vivo distribution of Arg-Ag@Cu and MMV-Arg-Ag@Cu in ICR mice with breast cancer lung metastasis. (A) Representative NIR fluorescence images in vivo imaging system of ICR mice with breast cancer lung metastasis at predetermined time points after intravenous injection with indicated formulation (λex = 750 nm). (B) The quantification of the NIR fluorescence signal intensity in lungs at indicated time points after indicated treatment. (C) The fluorescence signals of ICG in tumor area and main organs of mice treated with Arg-Ag@Cu or MMV-Arg-Ag@Cu at 6 h (n = 3/group, ∗p < 0.05, ∗∗p < 0.01, ∗∗∗p < 0.001, ∗∗∗∗p < 0.0001).

The therapeutic effect of Arg-Ag@Cu and MMV-Arg-Ag@Cu-DOX. (A) Flow cytometric analysis of apoptosis in 4T1 cells after indicated treatment. Q1 (AnnexinV-FITC-/PI+), dead cells; Q2 (AnnexinV-FITC+/PI+), late apoptotic cells; Q3 (AnnexinV-FITC+/PI−), early apoptotic cells; and Q4 (AnnexinV-FITC-/PI−), viable cells. Apoptotic cells were considered as Q2 and Q3. (B) The quantification of apoptotic index in 4T1 cells after indicated treatment. (C) The expression of apoptosis-associated proteins (caspase 3, Bcl-2 and Bax) in 4T1 cells after indicated treatment were determined by WB. (D) Quantitative analyses of the expression of each protein after indicated treatment. (n = 3/group) (E) The specific process of treatment. (F) Typical photographs of lungs from mice after treated with PBS, DOX, Arg-Ag@Cu-DOX, MMV-Arg-Ag@Cu-DOX and MMV-Arg-Ag@Cu-DOX + Laser, the crimson areas represented the visual necrotic tissue in each lung (scale bar = 1 cm). (G) the scheme of the detailed therapeutic mechanisms. (H) Quantitative analysis of tumor necrosis area after indicated treatment. (I) Recording the weight changes of mice. (J) Histological analysis of the lung tissues (scale bar = 200 μm). (n = 5/group, ∗p < 0.05, ∗∗p < 0.01, ∗∗∗p < 0.001, ∗∗∗∗p < 0.0001).

Effects and mechanism of Arg-Ag@Cu on 4T1 cells migration. (A) Cells migrated into the wounded region were imaged by IFM at 0, 6, 12, and 24 h (scale bar = 200 μm). (B) The area of the scratched wound was quantified (n = 3/group). (C) Representative photographs of transwell migration assay with 4T1 cells in the presence or without pre-treatment of Arg-Ag@Cu (scale bar = 200 μm). (D) The OD value measurement of crystal violet eluate of PBS and Arg-Ag@Cu treatment group were recorded and quantified (n = 3/group). (E) The expression of MMP-9, MMP-2 and TIMP-1, TIMP-2 was analyzed by WB. (F) Quantitative analyses of proteins after indicated treatment, compared with the control group. (n = 3/group) (G) The specific process of treatment. (H) Typical photographs of lung from mice treated with PBS and Arg-Ag@Cu, the crimson areas represent the visually necrotic tissue in each lung (scale bar = 1 cm). (I) Quantitative analysis of tumor necrosis area after indicated treatment. (J) Histological examination of lung tissues after H&E staining (scale bar = 200 μm). The white arrows denote the tumor lesions. (K) Recording the weight change of mice every day from the first day of treatment. (n = 5/group, ∗∗p < 0.01, ∗∗∗p < 0.001, ∗∗∗∗p < 0.0001).
Schematic diagram of the design and synthesis process of the duplex metal co-doped carbon dots-based drug delivery system and its mechanism for cancer theranostics.
